author: barnabasbusa
content:
Served engine_newPayloadV5  err="invalid argument 0: json: cannot unmarshal string
into Go struct field ExecutableData.blockAccessList of type bal.BlockAccessList"

author: mariusvanderwijden
content: Should be fixed, please try again

author: tbenr
content: We are aware, we currently do not serve finalized envelopes via rest

author: nflaig
content: this is strange, are you sure that's us doing something wrong? for us these are just opaque bytes, so empty BAL is expected if there are 0 state changes or 0 transaction in the payload?

author: b.hartnett
content: Yeah if there are no state changes for whatever reason thats fine but the correct encoding for an empty BAL is 0xc0 the empty rlp list. See the EIP https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7928#block-structure-modification where it says: `The BAL is RLP-encoded as a list of AccountChanges. When no state changes are present, this field is the empty RLP list 0xc0, i.e. rlp.encode([]).`

author: nflaig
content: but I don't understand why that's a CL responsibility, shouldn't the EL set the `0xc0` if BAL is empty?

author: b.hartnett
content: This was received via a newPayloadv5 which sends a ExecutionPayloadV4 which defines the blockAccessList as required. No in this case its the CL responsibility to send a correct BAL. There will be hive tests covering this soon so all ELs will reject in this case anyway: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/3082

author: nflaig
content: do you know which payload this was? for us we just handle the BAL as opaque bytes, and when we pass it to the EL we hex encode it, like we do with any other fields we pass to the EL
